title: Your Precon Is a Passport
description: A precon isn't a lesser deck you apologize for — it's the fastest legal way into a Commander game. The nerves aren't the problem. Not having four sentences ready is.
promise: You bought a precon and now you don't know what to say about it. Here are the four sentences that fix that.

title: Izzet and Prismari Are Both Blue-Red. They're Not the Same Deck.
description: Same two colors, same color pie, completely different game. Izzet and Prismari prove that a color identity tells you the toolbox, not which tool gets picked up twice a turn.
promise: Same wedge of the color wheel. Two different games underneath it.

title: Traceable Isn't the Same as True
description: A v1 launch scan on Vox Mana came back green — records existed, citations resolved, generated pages matched their source, regression passed. Exploratory testing found the checks were proving the wrong thing anyway. Here's the gap, and the rule I use to close it.
promise: A green scan proves a build is consistent with its own data. It doesn't prove the data was ever true.

title: A Quiz Can Be Evidence, Not Vibes
description: Most fandom quizzes hand you a confident label and no receipts. Building voxmana.io's placement quiz, I built the other kind — one that tracks its evidence, resolves the near-miss, and refuses to call a vibe a fact.
promise: A confident label with no receipts isn't a result. It's a costume.

title: Static Doesn’t Mean Safe — What Building voxmana.io Taught Me
description: Building voxmana.io as a static site didn't remove release risk — it hid it in the seams. Here's where a 'static' side project can still bite, and how I made those risks visible before launch.
promise: A static site has no server to crash. That's exactly why its riskiest failures don't announce themselves.

title: How I Cut a Regression Cycle in Half
description: Our platform upgraded twice a year, and testing one upgrade in full took three months. Here's the method that cut it to six weeks: an honest audit, a coverage map, a business-built rubric, vendor guidance, and ISTQB-style risk scoring.
promise: The lever wasn't more machines or more people. It was deciding, on purpose, what not to run.

title: The Ages of Fixing: What Every Magic Land Is Charging You
description: Thirty years of Magic land design is one long argument about a single question: how easy should it be to cast your spells on time? Read the lands as answers and it starts to make sense.
promise: The hand looked keepable until it didn't. Nobody countered me. Nobody outplayed me. I just couldn't cast my spells on time.

title: How I Run AI Like a QA System
description: AI is non-deterministic by design. I run my AI work the way I run quality engineering: operating prompts, grounding, examples, gates, and feedback loops.
promise: Most people make AI reliable by wording the prompt better. I make it reliable the way QA makes anything reliable — with systems.

title: What Good QA Actually Owns
description: QA owns quality — and you own it by making it visible to the right people, in the language they can act on, before it ships.
promise: QA owns quality. You own it by making it visible — and reading the room.
